D9 Biology L5 Evaluate Reference

C3 vs C4 vs CAM — Same Light, Different Geometry

three CO₂-fixing strategies — C3 (default), C4 (Kranz anatomy + spatial separation), CAM (succulent temporal separation) — same chlorophyll, drastically different water economy

C3 (most plants): RuBisCO fixes CO₂ in the mesophyll, but suffers photorespiration in hot conditions. C4 (corn, sugarcane): CO₂ first concentrated by PEP carboxylase in mesophyll cells, then released to RuBisCO in bundle-sheath cells (Kranz anatomy). CAM (cactus, pineapple): CO₂ fixed at night, stored as malate, released by day with stomata closed — saves water in deserts.

Toggle strategy. The leaf cross-section schematic + the per-stomata water-use bar + CO₂ flux pathway change.
